theory.canonical_statement: The Fibonacci sequence obeys F\_n = F\_\{n-1\} + F\_\{n-2\} with F\_0 = 0 and F\_1 = 1. The ratio of successive terms converges to the golden ratio $\phi$ = (1 + sqrt5)/2. A closed-form expression is F\_n = ($\phi$\^{}n -- $\psi$\^{}n)/sqrt5 where $\psi$ = (1 -- sqrt5)/2.
theory.technical_interpretation: The Fibonacci sequence obeys F\_n = F\_\{n-1\} + F\_\{n-2\} with F\_0 = 0 and F\_1 = 1. The ratio of successive terms converges to the golden ratio $\phi$ = (1 + sqrt5)/2. A closed-form expression is F\_n = ($\phi$\^{}n -- $\psi$\^{}n)/sqrt5 where $\psi$ = (1 -- sqrt5)/2.

equation_progression.interpretive_role: This introduces Fibonacci growth as the simplest cumulative memory law.
This converts the recurrence into an analytic expression governed by two exponential branches.
This closes the progression by identifying the asymptotic scale ratio that organizes the sequence.

external_sources.title: Fibonacci Sequence
external_sources.exact_language: The Heliyon paper defines the Fibonacci sequence and discusses its historyhttps://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6351390/#:~:text=Fibonacci%20numbers%20or%20Fibonacci%20sequence
external_sources.remaining_gap: modified%20golden%20ratio%20are%20presented. The Whitman College notes state the initial terms and recursion u_n = u_{n-1} + u_{n-2}www.whitman.edu/Documents/Academics/Mathematics/clancy.pdf#:~:text=The%20term%20%E2%80%9CFibonacci%20numbers%E2%80%9D%20is
external_sources.source_title: Fibonacci numbers: A population dynamics perspective
external_sources.relevance: The article reviews the history and mathematical definition of the Fibonacci sequence. It explains that the Fibonacci sequence is defined by the pattern 0, 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, … and discusses its early development and the recursive relation that defines the sequencehttps://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6351390/#:~:text=Fibonacci%20numbers%20or%20Fibonacci%20sequence,modified%20golden%20ratio%20are%20presented.
